author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5472 curious if anyone has some thoughts, maybe <@592004585506340885> or <@755590043632140352>? the current spec gives out of protocol builders that are connected via api quite an unfair advantage because of the p2p restriction we have. the trade-off here only seems to be increase of dos risk but as noted in the PR, to me this seems almost negligible since you can easily set up multiple onchain builders, so I think the current p2p rule mostly hurts honest builders

author: potuz
content: First thought is that it's unnecessary complexity that may open the path for DOS on a global topic that any one can post. Doesn't seem that dangerous though and I am not strongly against it but I don't think it gives any meaningful gain at the cost of extra P2P complexity.

author: nflaig
content: I did a poc on lodestar, since we already have `(bid.slot, bid.parent_block_hash, bid.parent_block_root)` seen cache it was kinda trivial at least in terms of implementation

author: potuz
content: Yeah I don't think it's that difficult.
